title: navarretia densifolia subsp. elongata
intro: Navarretia densifolia subsp. elongata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to California, United States. It is also known as the elongated navarretia or purple owl's clover, and is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Navarretia densifolia subsp. elongata include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as possessing antimicrobial properties that may help to combat infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea or tincture, and may also be added to salves or ointments to take advantage of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
